title: Citrusy Flaky Dutch Sausage Bitterballen
description: A show-off Dutch dish featuring thyme and a hint of citrus, perfect for a special occasion. This egg-free-friendly recipe is sure to impress.

ingredients.name: 2 egg yolks (replaced with flaxseed egg mixture in this recipe)
flaxseed meal
ingredients.notes: mixed with 3 tablespoons water to replace egg yolks

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, thyme, salt, and pepper.
Gradually add the water and mix until the dough comes together.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until smooth.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and rest for 30 minutes.
Cut the pork sausages into small pieces and add to the dough, mixing until well combined.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces.
Roll each piece into a ball and then flatten into a disk shape.
Heat the melted butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add a few of the dough disks to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
Drain the bitterballen on paper towels and serve hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
For the flaxseed egg mixture, mix 1 tablespoon of flaxseed meal with 3 tablespoons of water and let sit for 5 minutes before using.
